description: 'nosa' locates an act or condition at the current time. Although it is a noun, it commonly occupies the adverbial position in a clause. 'nomi' instead makes the current span or a state of presence the subject, while 'nosa' answers when something is happening.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and carry nasal airflow into stressed middle-height rounded 'o'. Bring the tongue blade near the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's', then open the jaw into central 'a'.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: 'nosa' lets a speaker return an utterance to the act or condition happening at this time, the temporal anchor of present attention.

semantic_domains.temporal: the current time used as the location of an act or condition
